briefing.opening: Hello everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 13, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensification of the Climate Crisis and International Warnings
sections.content: In today's climate news, the direct impacts of the globally intensifying climate crisis and warning messages regarding it were prominent. In Brazil's coffee belt, torrential rainfall described as a 'water bomb' has occurred, threatening agricultural production [2], while domestically, vulnerable populations are experiencing real harm, such as seniors facing annual outdoor activity restrictions of 900 hours due to severe heatwaves [5]. Internationally, research findings suggesting that global warming could accelerate have been published, and experts' concerns are growing regarding the adverse effects of climate change on human health, including extended allergy seasons and an increase in inflammatory diseases [111, 112, 116]. Notably, with temperatures rising by 2.5 degrees Celsius over the past 50 years, the perceived pace of climate change is accelerating, as evidenced by melting ice causing difficulties in Winter Paralympic events [121], and former defense leaders have warned that oil wars threaten security and climate change exacerbates risks [124].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Expansion of Domestic Carbon Neutrality Policies and Practices
sections.content: Domestically, the policy framework for achieving carbon neutrality is being strengthened, and citizen participation is actively taking place. Assemblyman Wi Seong-gon proposed an amendment to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act, which seeks to legislate a 'carbon budget,' and this amendment, including provisions such as specifying the obligation to protect climate-vulnerable groups, passed the National Assembly [6, 10, 15, 25]. Incheon City held an integrated carbon neutrality committee meeting, presenting the blueprint for its 2026 environmental policies [7, 53], and various local governments, including Daegu, Daejeon, and Gangneung, are recruiting participants for the automobile carbon neutrality point system, encouraging citizens' voluntary carbon reduction activities [8, 12, 14, 18, 24, 28, 29, 33, 34, 37, 41, 45, 49, 51, 55, 56, 57, 60]. Furthermore, efforts to practice carbon neutrality are spreading across all sectors, including climate change response farming education by Goyang Byeokje Nonghyup [1], carbon neutrality action group activities for youth and seniors [9, 17, 20, 21, 27, 42, 48, 52, 58, 61, 62], and strengthened ecological transition education in schools [26, 39, 59].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating both domestically and internationally, but it simultaneously faces several challenges. Prompted by instability in the Middle East, the government and ruling party have announced plans to promote the expansion of renewable energy [66, 79, 85], and locally-led renewable energy projects are also active, such as Gwangyang City's resident-participatory solar power generation project [4, 80, 91, 102] and Taean County's offshore wind power investment agreement [90]. The Korea Industrial Complex Corporation (KICOX) is providing solar power consulting utilizing factory rooftops within industrial complexes to aid in their wider adoption [71, 92, 98]; however, urban solar power faces difficulties in widespread adoption due to regulations and high costs [84]. Meanwhile, the necessity for improving the Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) system has been raised [65, 83, 100], and policy challenges also exist in the energy transition process, such as policy conflicts between nuclear and renewable energy in France [68]. Furthermore, unfortunate incidents within new and renewable energy companies [73, 76, 81, 88, 89, 93, 95, 96, 97, 99, 103, 104] are raising concerns that they could undermine the industry's credibility and healthy growth.

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Action and Research Trends
sections.content: Globally, responses to and research on climate change are taking various forms. The New Orleans City Council held a meeting of its Climate Change and Sustainability Committee [109], and international cooperation discussions are ongoing, such as talks between Vanuatu and Japan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs on climate change adaptation [117]. The case of Bonaire has clarified the state's obligation to protect vulnerable areas from climate change [113], and movements holding corporations accountable for climate change are also active [123]. Meanwhile, in the scientific community, interesting insights into nature's adaptation methods have been presented, such as packrat middens revealing climate change information [114], or research findings indicating that wild plants can rapidly evolve to 'save' themselves from climate change [115, 118, 119]. In Brazil, research on new coffee trees resistant to climate change is underway [122], and discussions are also taking place on better ways to measure individual climate action [120].

sections.title: 📰 Energy Market and Industry Changes
sections.content: Responding to the climate crisis is triggering structural changes across the energy market and industry. In Pohang, a forum for senior public officials was held, inviting the Chairman of the Climate Change Center [3], and policymakers are enhancing their understanding of climate change response, as evidenced by the Daegu City Council's Economic and Environmental Committee's site visit to the Carbon Neutrality Support Center [11]. A revised industrial electricity tariff plan, reflecting the domestic power situation, has been unveiled, lowering daytime rates and raising nighttime rates [107], indicating attempts to adjust market mechanisms to enhance energy efficiency. Furthermore, major industrial players are actively responding to these changes; POSCO Future M anticipates extending electric vehicle driving ranges through collaboration in advanced battery material technology development [106], and Korea Gas Corporation (KOGAS) is strengthening its ESG management through process efficiency improvements and the use of renewable energy [101].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing. This is a time that calls for a deep understanding of climate change and proactive responses. We look forward to seeing you again at the next briefing.
